title: Unique Umami Middle Eastern Ume Plum Tagine
description: A briny and dense festive Middle Eastern dish featuring ume-plum, simmered to perfection in a terracotta dish, with anti-inflammatory friendly ingredients.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Heat the olive oil in the terracotta dish over medium heat.
Add the chopped onions and cook until they are softened and translucent.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the ground cumin, coriander, and turmeric and cook for 1 minute.
Add the dried ume plums, chicken broth, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then cover the dish with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Simmer the tagine for 2 hours, or until the flavors have melded together and the ume plums are tender.
Remove the dish from the oven and let it cool slightly before serving.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
